{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** ** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} ** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** ** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} ** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** ** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} ** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Miyagi Prefecture]] The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** ** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} ** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Miyagi Prefecture]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** ** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} ** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Miyagi Prefecture]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** ** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} ** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural monuments in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]][[Category:qq]] (Sendai City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamo (Sendai City)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-qq-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-qq-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-qq-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** ** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/qqTENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-qq-08}} ** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Miyagi Prefecture]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-qq-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-qq-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-qq-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** ** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/qqTENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-qq-08}} ** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Miyagi Prefecture]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-qq-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-qq-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-qq-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** ** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/qqTENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-qq-08}} ** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** ** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} ** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Miyagi Prefecture]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Miyagi Prefecture]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Miyagi Prefecture]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots
