revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 賀茂神社 |画像 = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
左：上賀茂神社・右：下賀茂神社 |所在地 = [[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[泉区 (仙台市)|泉区]]古内字糺1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|display=title,inline}} |祭神 = （下賀茂神社）
[[鴨建角身命]]
[[多々須玉依姫]]
（上賀茂神社）
[[別雷命]] |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = 糺さま、赤神さま |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月29日]]（[[5月1日]]） |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} {{座標一覧}} '''賀茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[泉区 (仙台市)|泉区]]古内[[小字|字]]糺にある[[神社]]。'''下賀茂'''・'''上賀茂'''の2社が並立する形式をとる。 [[仙台藩家臣]]・古内氏の屋敷跡にあり、当地に遷宮する前の名称から「糺（ただす）さま」、社殿の色から「赤神さま」とも呼ばれる {{Cite book|和書 |editor=朝日新聞仙台総局|editor-link=朝日新聞 |year=2008 |title=新・散歩みち 宮城おさんぽガイド88 |publisher=荒蝦夷 |isbn= }} 。 == 概要 == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|賀茂神社の拝殿前にある八咫烏神社]] [[泉中央 (仙台市)|泉中央]]地区から西方にあり、境内の北側を[[七北田川]]（冠川）が蛇行しながら東流している。この七北田川と[[七北田丘陵]]に挟まれた狭い[[河岸段丘]]の際[http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html 賀茂神社本殿 附 棟札2枚]（仙台市）に境内はあり、西に上賀茂、東に下賀茂の両社が並んで[[南東]]を向いて鎮座している。その社殿の様式は茅葺箱棟造（かやぶきはこむねづくり）である。 下賀茂神社の正式名称は、'''賀茂御祖神社'''（かもみおやじんじゃ、{{ウィキ座標|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|地図|name=賀茂御祖神社（下賀茂神社）}}）。祭神は、[[鴨建角身命]]（かもたけつねみのみこと）と[[多々須玉依姫]]（ただちたまよりひめ）の2柱。地元では「東の宮」と呼ばれる。 上賀茂神社の正式名称は、'''賀茂別雷神社'''（かもわけいかづちじんじゃ、{{ウィキ座標|38|19|15.9|N|140|50|56.2|E|region:JP|地図|name=賀茂別雷神社（上賀茂神社）}}）。[[別雷命]]（わけいかづちのみこと）を祭る。地元では「西の宮」と呼ばれる。 その他、'''[[八咫烏]]神社'''（やたがらすじんじゃ）も境内に所在する。 神社の周囲には巨木や古木が多く残り、仙台市の「杜の都・仙台 令和版 わがまち緑の名所100選」に選ばれている{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf |title=杜の都・仙台 令和版 わがまち緑の名所100選【泉区の名所】賀茂神社 |publisher=仙台市 |accessdate=2024-11-08 }}。 住所の[[小字]]は当地に遷宮する前の当社の名称から糺となっているが、[[大字]]の古内は当地が古内氏の領地であったことと関係している。古内氏の中では[[古内義如|古内志摩義如]]が著名で、[[仙台藩]]4代藩主[[伊達綱村]]のときの国老（[[家老]]）であり、[[1671年]]（[[寛文]]11年）の[[伊達騒動#寛文事件|寛文事件]]では刃傷沙汰の現場に居合わせた者の中で唯一の生き残りとなった。[[1673年]]（[[延宝]]元年）に43歳で没し、志摩の屋敷前の慈眼寺に葬られた。当社は志摩の屋敷跡に建てられたものである。 == 歴史 == [[鹽竈神社]]の神職が、[[陸奥国]][[宮城郡]][[利府町|利府村]]野中男鹿島（{{Coord|38|19|26.8|N|140|59|1.5|E|region:JP|name=男鹿島のおおよその位置}}）現在の[[宮城県]]宮城郡[[利府町]]男鹿島と考えられる。にあった自らの屋敷内に只洲宮および[[貴布禰神社 (仙台市)|貴船宮]][http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020340 貴布禰神社]（宮城県[[神社庁]]）を祀っていたが、この只洲宮が現在の下賀茂神社の起源とされている[http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm 由緒]（賀茂神社）。のちに貴船宮は鹽竈神社境内に遷され、[[1607年]]（[[慶長]]12年）には只洲宮（糺（ただす）の宮）も鹽竈神社（{{Coord|38|19|8.1|N|141|0|45.4|E|region:JP|name=鹽竈神社}}）境内に遷された。[[仙台藩]]第3代藩主[[伊達綱宗]]が、[[伊達騒動]]で既に子の綱村に家督を譲っていたものの[[1663年]]（[[寛文]]3年）に鹽竈神社の修造を行い、只洲宮と貴船宮は鹽竈神社境内で遷宮された。仙台藩第4代藩主[[伊達綱村]]による鹽竈神社の修造の際には、只洲宮と貴船宮は鹽竈神社の外の別々の地に遷宮されることになり、[[1673年]]（[[延宝]]元年）に没した[[古内義如|古内志摩]]の屋敷跡である当地[http://www.stks.city.sendai.jp/sgks/WebPages/izumiku/42/42-12.htm 七北田の名所と旧跡案内]（仙台市中央市民センター）に、[[1696年]]（[[元禄]]9年）[http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020340 宮城県神社庁の貴布禰神社のページ]によると、『風土記御用所』では貴布禰神社の遷宮年月が不詳としている。、只洲宮は下賀茂神社として遷宮した。同時に上賀茂神社と八咫烏神社も[[勧請]]された。 === 年表 === * [[1607年]]（[[慶長]]12年） - 利府村から[[鹽竈神社]]境内に遷宮。 * [[1663年]]（[[寛文]]3年） - 鹽竈神社境内で遷宮。 * [[1696年]][[4月1日]]（[[元禄]]9年[[2月29日 (旧暦)|2月29日]]） - 現在地にて着工。 * 1696年[[10月18日]]（元禄9年[[9月23日 (旧暦)|9月23日]]） - '''御祖神社'''（下賀茂神社）として奉齋。 * [[1697年]][[2月20日]]（元禄10年[[1月29日 (旧暦)|1月29日]]） - '''別雷神社'''（上賀茂神社）として奉齋。 == 祭典 == どんと祭の時には裸参りも行われ、周辺道路が交通規制される。 * 歳旦祭（1月1日） * [[どんと祭]]（1月14日）参拝者数：約3万人 * 春季例祭（4月29日） * 夏越大祓式（7月31日） * 秋季例祭（10月7日） * 七五三祝祭（11月） == 文化財 == [[File:Acer palmatum, Kamo Shrine, Sendai, Japan.JPG|thumb|220px|賀茂神社（仙台）のイロハモミジ]] * 宮城県指定有形文化財（[[1964年]]（[[昭和]]39年）[[9月4日]]指定） ** 下賀茂神社・上賀茂神社本殿二棟[http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/01kenzoubutu/ken/09kamo.htm 賀茂神社本殿]（宮城県） * 宮城県指定天然記念物（[[1999年]]（[[平成]]11年）[[7月2日]]指定） ** [[イロハモミジ]]2本[http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm 賀茂神社のイロハモミジ]（宮城県） ** [[タラヨウ]]1本 * 仙台市指定天然記念物（[[1985年]]（昭和60年）[[4月25日]]に[[泉市]]が指定し、同市を編入合併した仙台市が再指定） ** [[アラカシ]]2本[http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/c0000000169.html 賀茂神社のアラカシ]（仙台市） == アクセス == * [[ファイル:Sendai City Subway Logo.svg|30px|link=仙台市地下鉄]] [[仙台市地下鉄]][[仙台市地下鉄南北線|南北線]]・[[八乙女駅]]下車、当駅[[バスターミナル]]から[[宮城交通|宮城交通バス]]に乗車、「賀茂神社」バス停で下車し、徒歩で約1分。 * [[ファイル:NEXCO_East.png|30px|link=東日本高速道路]] [[東北自動車道]]・[[泉パーキングエリア|泉PA]][[スマートインターチェンジ|スマートIC]]で下りて、[[宮城県道35号泉塩釜線]]を西進し、[[宮城県道264号大衡仙台線]]に入って南進。東北自動車道を跨ぐ陸橋を越えたら信号で東進して左側。 == 周辺 == 同社の南側にある[[七北田丘陵]]には、[[中世]]の城跡の[[長命館]][http://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/081/081.html 長命館跡]（仙台市 [[杜の都]] 緑の名所100選）[http://www.stks.city.sendai.jp/sgks/WebPages/izumiku/43/43-03.htm 長命館落城と蝦夷国家の滅亡]（仙台市中央市民センター）があり、周囲の丘陵部は加茂団地として開発されている。 門前の道通称は古内道。正式名称は仙台市道泉4004号・加茂幹線2号線は、七北田川南岸沿いに[[泉ヶ岳]]東麓に至る道で、北岸沿いの[[宮城県道35号泉塩釜線]]と共に七北田川沿いの主要道であったが、[[宮城県道37号仙台北環状線]]等が完成してからは裏道になった。 同社の門前には、[[長命沼]]から流れ出る水路を挟んで[[臨済宗]]の盛徳山[[慈眼寺]]（{{Coord|38|19|10.2|N|140|51|2.2|E|region:JP|name=慈眼寺}}）がある。[[北山五山]]の1つである[[資福寺]]の[[末寺]]である。同寺は[[古内義如|古内志摩義如]]が、[[1655年]]（明暦元年）に死んだ母（[[横沢将監]]の娘）を葬るために建立したものである[http://www.stks.city.sendai.jp/sgks/WebPages/izumiku/48/48-21.htm (4) 長命ヶ丘市民センターから北東へ 賀茂神社・長命館公園方面]（仙台市中央市民センター）。慈眼寺という名称は、志摩の父の[[戒名|法名]]に由来する。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="※"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja, Sendai}} * [[宮城県の観光地]] * [[賀茂神社]] * [[加茂神社]] * [[八咫烏]] * [[熊野三山]] * [[宮城県の神社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kamojinja.org/ 賀茂神社]（公式[[ウェブサイト]]） * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html 賀茂神社]（仙台市 杜の都 緑の名所100選） * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/izumi/soumu/konjaku/07huruuti.html 第7回 古内めぐり・古内道路を歩く]（仙台市泉区「いずみ史跡今昔物語」） * [http://www.stks.city.sendai.jp/sgks/WebPages/izumiku/48/48-21.htm (4) 長命ヶ丘市民センターから北東へ 賀茂神社・長命館公園方面]（仙台市中央市民センター） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもしんしや}} [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:仙台市泉区の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市泉区の歴史]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:宮城県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:宮城県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:加茂神社|せんたいし]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] to [[Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 賀茂神社 |画像 = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
左：上賀茂神社・右：下賀茂神社 |所在地 = [[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[泉区 (仙台市)|泉区]]古内字糺1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|display=title,inline}} |祭神 = （下賀茂神社）
[[鴨建角身命]]
[[多々須玉依姫]]
（上賀茂神社）
[[別雷命]] |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = 糺さま、赤神さま |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月29日]]（[[5月1日]]） |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} {{座標一覧}} '''賀茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[泉区 (仙台市)|泉区]]古内[[小字|字]]糺にある[[神社]]。'''下賀茂'''・'''上賀茂'''の2社が並立する形式をとる。 [[仙台藩家臣]]・古内氏の屋敷跡にあり、当地に遷宮する前の名称から「糺（ただす）さま」、社殿の色から「赤神さま」とも呼ばれる {{Cite book|和書 |editor=朝日新聞仙台総局|editor-link=朝日新聞 |year=2008 |title=新・散歩みち 宮城おさんぽガイド88 |publisher=荒蝦夷 |isbn= }} 。 == 概要 == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|賀茂神社の拝殿前にある八咫烏神社]] [[泉中央 (仙台市)|泉中央]]地区から西方にあり、境内の北側を[[七北田川]]（冠川）が蛇行しながら東流している。この七北田川と[[七北田丘陵]]に挟まれた狭い[[河岸段丘]]の際[http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html 賀茂神社本殿 附 棟札2枚]（仙台市）に境内はあり、西に上賀茂、東に下賀茂の両社が並んで[[南東]]を向いて鎮座している。その社殿の様式は茅葺箱棟造（かやぶきはこむねづくり）である。 下賀茂神社の正式名称は、'''賀茂御祖神社'''（かもみおやじんじゃ、{{ウィキ座標|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|地図|name=賀茂御祖神社（下賀茂神社）}}）。祭神は、[[鴨建角身命]]（かもたけつねみのみこと）と[[多々須玉依姫]]（ただちたまよりひめ）の2柱。地元では「東の宮」と呼ばれる。 上賀茂神社の正式名称は、'''賀茂別雷神社'''（かもわけいかづちじんじゃ、{{ウィキ座標|38|19|15.9|N|140|50|56.2|E|region:JP|地図|name=賀茂別雷神社（上賀茂神社）}}）。[[別雷命]]（わけいかづちのみこと）を祭る。地元では「西の宮」と呼ばれる。 その他、'''[[八咫烏]]神社'''（やたがらすじんじゃ）も境内に所在する。 神社の周囲には巨木や古木が多く残り、仙台市の「杜の都・仙台 令和版 わがまち緑の名所100選」に選ばれている{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf |title=杜の都・仙台 令和版 わがまち緑の名所100選【泉区の名所】賀茂神社 |publisher=仙台市 |accessdate=2024-11-08 }}。 住所の[[小字]]は当地に遷宮する前の当社の名称から糺となっているが、[[大字]]の古内は当地が古内氏の領地であったことと関係している。古内氏の中では[[古内義如|古内志摩義如]]が著名で、[[仙台藩]]4代藩主[[伊達綱村]]のときの国老（[[家老]]）であり、[[1671年]]（[[寛文]]11年）の[[伊達騒動#寛文事件|寛文事件]]では刃傷沙汰の現場に居合わせた者の中で唯一の生き残りとなった。[[1673年]]（[[延宝]]元年）に43歳で没し、志摩の屋敷前の慈眼寺に葬られた。当社は志摩の屋敷跡に建てられたものである。 == 歴史 == [[鹽竈神社]]の神職が、[[陸奥国]][[宮城郡]][[利府町|利府村]]野中男鹿島（{{Coord|38|19|26.8|N|140|59|1.5|E|region:JP|name=男鹿島のおおよその位置}}）現在の[[宮城県]]宮城郡[[利府町]]男鹿島と考えられる。にあった自らの屋敷内に只洲宮および[[貴布禰神社 (仙台市)|貴船宮]][http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020340 貴布禰神社]（宮城県[[神社庁]]）を祀っていたが、この只洲宮が現在の下賀茂神社の起源とされている[http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm 由緒]（賀茂神社）。のちに貴船宮は鹽竈神社境内に遷され、[[1607年]]（[[慶長]]12年）には只洲宮（糺（ただす）の宮）も鹽竈神社（{{Coord|38|19|8.1|N|141|0|45.4|E|region:JP|name=鹽竈神社}}）境内に遷された。[[仙台藩]]第3代藩主[[伊達綱宗]]が、[[伊達騒動]]で既に子の綱村に家督を譲っていたものの[[1663年]]（[[寛文]]3年）に鹽竈神社の修造を行い、只洲宮と貴船宮は鹽竈神社境内で遷宮された。仙台藩第4代藩主[[伊達綱村]]による鹽竈神社の修造の際には、只洲宮と貴船宮は鹽竈神社の外の別々の地に遷宮されることになり、[[1673年]]（[[延宝]]元年）に没した[[古内義如|古内志摩]]の屋敷跡である当地[http://www.stks.city.sendai.jp/sgks/WebPages/izumiku/42/42-12.htm 七北田の名所と旧跡案内]（仙台市中央市民センター）に、[[1696年]]（[[元禄]]9年）[http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020340 宮城県神社庁の貴布禰神社のページ]によると、『風土記御用所』では貴布禰神社の遷宮年月が不詳としている。、只洲宮は下賀茂神社として遷宮した。同時に上賀茂神社と八咫烏神社も[[勧請]]された。 === 年表 === * [[1607年]]（[[慶長]]12年） - 利府村から[[鹽竈神社]]境内に遷宮。 * [[1663年]]（[[寛文]]3年） - 鹽竈神社境内で遷宮。 * [[1696年]][[4月1日]]（[[元禄]]9年[[2月29日 (旧暦)|2月29日]]） - 現在地にて着工。 * 1696年[[10月18日]]（元禄9年[[9月23日 (旧暦)|9月23日]]） - '''御祖神社'''（下賀茂神社）として奉齋。 * [[1697年]][[2月20日]]（元禄10年[[1月29日 (旧暦)|1月29日]]） - '''別雷神社'''（上賀茂神社）として奉齋。 == 祭典 == どんと祭の時には裸参りも行われ、周辺道路が交通規制される。 * 歳旦祭（1月1日） * [[どんと祭]]（1月14日）参拝者数：約3万人 * 春季例祭（4月29日） * 夏越大祓式（7月31日） * 秋季例祭（10月7日） * 七五三祝祭（11月） == 文化財 == [[File:Acer palmatum, Kamo Shrine, Sendai, Japan.JPG|thumb|220px|賀茂神社（仙台）のイロハモミジ]] * 宮城県指定有形文化財（[[1964年]]（[[昭和]]39年）[[9月4日]]指定） ** 下賀茂神社・上賀茂神社本殿二棟[http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/01kenzoubutu/ken/09kamo.htm 賀茂神社本殿]（宮城県） * 宮城県指定天然記念物（[[1999年]]（[[平成]]11年）[[7月2日]]指定） ** [[イロハモミジ]]2本[http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm 賀茂神社のイロハモミジ]（宮城県） ** [[タラヨウ]]1本 * 仙台市指定天然記念物（[[1985年]]（昭和60年）[[4月25日]]に[[泉市]]が指定し、同市を編入合併した仙台市が再指定） ** [[アラカシ]]2本[http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/c0000000169.html 賀茂神社のアラカシ]（仙台市） == アクセス == * [[ファイル:Sendai City Subway Logo.svg|30px|link=仙台市地下鉄]] [[仙台市地下鉄]][[仙台市地下鉄南北線|南北線]]・[[八乙女駅]]下車、当駅[[バスターミナル]]から[[宮城交通|宮城交通バス]]に乗車、「賀茂神社」バス停で下車し、徒歩で約1分。 * [[ファイル:NEXCO_East.png|30px|link=東日本高速道路]] [[東北自動車道]]・[[泉パーキングエリア|泉PA]][[スマートインターチェンジ|スマートIC]]で下りて、[[宮城県道35号泉塩釜線]]を西進し、[[宮城県道264号大衡仙台線]]に入って南進。東北自動車道を跨ぐ陸橋を越えたら信号で東進して左側。 == 周辺 == 同社の南側にある[[七北田丘陵]]には、[[中世]]の城跡の[[長命館]][http://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/081/081.html 長命館跡]（仙台市 [[杜の都]] 緑の名所100選）[http://www.stks.city.sendai.jp/sgks/WebPages/izumiku/43/43-03.htm 長命館落城と蝦夷国家の滅亡]（仙台市中央市民センター）があり、周囲の丘陵部は加茂団地として開発されている。 門前の道通称は古内道。正式名称は仙台市道泉4004号・加茂幹線2号線は、七北田川南岸沿いに[[泉ヶ岳]]東麓に至る道で、北岸沿いの[[宮城県道35号泉塩釜線]]と共に七北田川沿いの主要道であったが、[[宮城県道37号仙台北環状線]]等が完成してからは裏道になった。 同社の門前には、[[長命沼]]から流れ出る水路を挟んで[[臨済宗]]の盛徳山[[慈眼寺]]（{{Coord|38|19|10.2|N|140|51|2.2|E|region:JP|name=慈眼寺}}）がある。[[北山五山]]の1つである[[資福寺]]の[[末寺]]である。同寺は[[古内義如|古内志摩義如]]が、[[1655年]]（明暦元年）に死んだ母（[[横沢将監]]の娘）を葬るために建立したものである[http://www.stks.city.sendai.jp/sgks/WebPages/izumiku/48/48-21.htm (4) 長命ヶ丘市民センターから北東へ 賀茂神社・長命館公園方面]（仙台市中央市民センター）。慈眼寺という名称は、志摩の父の[[戒名|法名]]に由来する。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="※"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja, Sendai}} * [[宮城県の観光地]] * [[賀茂神社]] * [[加茂神社]] * [[八咫烏]] * [[熊野三山]] * [[宮城県の神社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kamojinja.org/ 賀茂神社]（公式[[ウェブサイト]]） * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html 賀茂神社]（仙台市 杜の都 緑の名所100選） * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/izumi/soumu/konjaku/07huruuti.html 第7回 古内めぐり・古内道路を歩く]（仙台市泉区「いずみ史跡今昔物語」） * [http://www.stks.city.sendai.jp/sgks/WebPages/izumiku/48/48-21.htm (4) 長命ヶ丘市民センターから北東へ 賀茂神社・長命館公園方面]（仙台市中央市民センター） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもしんしや}} [[Category:仙台市の神社]] [[Category:仙台市泉区の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市泉区の歴史]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:宮城県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:宮城県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:加茂神社|せんたいし]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine Rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural monuments in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[sn:amo Shrine (Sendai City)]] {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (仙台市)|version=102499368|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine Rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭|12=draft}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine Rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto-sai|ja|どんと祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Donto-sai|qq=}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (仙台市)|version=102499368|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural monuments in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[sn:amo Shrine (Sendai City)]]

Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine Rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto Festival|ja|どんと祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Donto-sai|qq=}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (仙台市)|version=102499368|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural monuments in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[sn:amo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[Category:仙台市泉区の建築物]] Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto Festival|ja|どんと祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Donto-sai|qq=}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (仙台市)|version=102499368|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in [[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine Rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto Festival|ja|どんと祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Donto-sai|qq=}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine Rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto Festival|ja|どんと祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Donto-sai|qq=}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (仙台市)|version=102499368|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Historic sites in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural monuments in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[sn:amo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[Category:仙台市泉区の建築物]] [[Category:仙台市泉区の歴史]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:宮城県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] {{Commons category|Kamo-jinja, Sendai}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine Rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[:en:April 29|April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto Festival|ja|どんと祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Donto-sai|qq=}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (仙台市)|version=102499368|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[sn:amo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[Category:仙台市泉区の建築物]] {{Commons category|Kamo-jinja, Sendai}}

The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[:en:Date Tsunamura|Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto Festival|ja|どんと祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Donto-sai|qq=}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = [[File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg|270px]]
Left: Kamigamo Shrine, Right: Shimogamo Shrine | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine Rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[:en:April 29|April 29]] (or [[May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[:en:Date Tsunamura|Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto Festival|ja|どんと祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Donto-sai|qq=}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (仙台市)|version=102499368|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[sn:amo Shrine (Sendai City)]] [[Category:Architecture in Izumi Ward, Sendai]] [[Category:History of Izumi Ward, Sendai]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:宮城県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] {{Commons The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[:en:Date Tsunamura|Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto Festival|ja|どんと祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Donto-sai|qq=}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[:en:Yaotome Station|Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == [[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine Rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = [[:en:April 29|April 29]] (or [[:en:May 1|May 1]]) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the [[Sendai Domain]].{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, [[:en:Yatagarasu|Yatagarasu]]. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from [[Shiogama Shrine]] in the [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]], the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under [[:en:Date Tsunamura|Date Tsunamura]], the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto Festival|ja|どんと祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Donto-sai|qq=}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** [[Sendai Subway Namboku Line]] to [[:en:Yaotome Station|Yaotome Station]], followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. ==
