revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja, Sendai]][[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja, Sendai]][[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja, Sendai]][[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja, Sendai]][[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38.321139|N|140.849056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine Rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29|1=April 29}} (or {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1|1=May 1}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} | prefecture= {{ill|Izumi-ku|WD=Q1043338|1=Izumi-ku, Sendai}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|en|Sendai Domain|WD=Q1062182|lt=Sendai Domain|1=Sendai Domain}}.{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu|1=Yatagarasu}}. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} in the {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of {{ill|Date Masamune|en|Date Masamune|WD=Q311183|lt=Date Masamune|1=Date Masamune}}, the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under {{ill|Date Tsunamura|en|Date Tsunamura|WD=Q5227557|lt=Date Tsunamura|1=Date Tsunamura}}, the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto Festival|ja|どんと祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Donto-sai|qq=}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** {{ill|Sendai Subway Namboku Line|en|Sendai Subway Namboku Line|WD=Q908156|lt=Sendai Subway Namboku Line|1=Sendai Subway Namboku Line}} to {{ill|Yaotome Station|en|Yaotome Station|WD=Q8049075|lt=Yaotome Station|1=Yaotome Station}}, followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[:en:Tohoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (仙台市)|version=102499368|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] {{Commons category|Kamo-jinja, Sendai}} {{wikidata link|Q11634948}} [[Category:Architecture in Izumi Ward, Sendai]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi Ward, Sendai]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja, Sendai]][[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja, Sendai]][[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja, Sendai]][[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kamo-jinja, Sendai]][[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38.321139|N|140.849056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine Rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29|1=April 29}} (or {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1|1=May 1}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} | prefecture= {{ill|Izumi-ku|WD=Q1043338|1=Izumi-ku, Sendai}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|en|Sendai Domain|WD=Q1062182|lt=Sendai Domain|1=Sendai Domain}}.{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu|1=Yatagarasu}}. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} in the {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of {{ill|Date Masamune|en|Date Masamune|WD=Q311183|lt=Date Masamune|1=Date Masamune}}, the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under {{ill|Date Tsunamura|en|Date Tsunamura|WD=Q5227557|lt=Date Tsunamura|1=Date Tsunamura}}, the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto Festival|ja|どんと祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Donto-sai|qq=}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** {{ill|Sendai Subway Namboku Line|en|Sendai Subway Namboku Line|WD=Q908156|lt=Sendai Subway Namboku Line|1=Sendai Subway Namboku Line}} to {{ill|Yaotome Station|en|Yaotome Station|WD=Q8049075|lt=Yaotome Station|1=Yaotome Station}}, followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[:en:Tohoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (仙台市)|version=102499368|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] {{Commons category|Kamo-jinja, Sendai}} {{wikidata link|Q11634948}} [[Category:Architecture in Izumi Ward, Sendai]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi Ward, Sendai]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja, Sendai]][[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja, Sendai]][[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja, Sendai]][[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] [[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Kamo Shrine | image = File:KamojinjaSendai-hondenKamiShimo.jpg | location = Furuchi Aza Tadasu 1, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|38.321139|N|140.849056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = (Shimogamo Shrine) [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]]
[[Tamakushi-hime]]
(Kamigamo Shrine) [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Kamo-wake-ikazuchi]] | shrine Rank = | established = Unknown | main hall = Single-bay [[nagare-zukuri]] style | aliases = Tadasu-sama, Akagami-sama | festivals = {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|qid=Q2535|lt=April 29|1=April 29}} (or {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|qid=Q2544|lt=May 1|1=May 1}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Miyagi|lat_deg=38|lat_min=19|lat_sec=16.1|lon_deg=140|lon_min=50|lon_sec=56.6|label=賀茂神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|38|19|16.1|N|140|50|56.6|E}}}} | prefecture= {{ill|Izumi-ku|qid=Q1043338|1=Izumi-ku, Sendai}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂神社|かもじんじゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Furuchi, Izumi Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. The shrine comprises two main shrines, '''Kamigamo Shrine''' and '''Shimogamo Shrine''', positioned side by side. Locally referred to as '''Tadasu-sama''' after its former location or '''Akagami-sama''' due to its red-colored buildings, Kamo Shrine is situated on the former estate of the Furuchi clan, retainers of the {{ill|Sendai Domain|en|Sendai Domain|qid=Q1062182|lt=Sendai Domain|1=Sendai Domain}}.{{Cite book|editor=Asahi Shimbun Sendai Bureau|title=New Walking Paths: 88 Scenic Spots in Miyagi|publisher=Arakawa Eizo|year=2008}} == Overview == [[File:KamojinjaSendai-yatagarasujinja.jpg|thumb|220px|Yatagarasu Shrine within the Kamo Shrine precincts]] Kamo Shrine is located west of the [[Izumi-Chūō]] area, along the northern bank of the [[Nanakita River]]. The shrine complex sits on a narrow river terrace between the Nanakita River and the surrounding hills.{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/database/k0000000035.html|title=Kamo Shrine main hall - Sendai City Cultural Properties|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} The shrine structures face southeast, with Shimogamo Shrine to the east and Kamigamo Shrine to the west. Both feature thatched roofing and a distinct architectural style. Shimogamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Mioya Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂御祖神社|かもみおやじんじゃ}}), enshrines [[Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamo Taketsunemi no Mikoto]] and [[Tamakushi-hime]]. Locals refer to it as "East Shrine." Kamigamo Shrine, formally '''Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine''' ({{nihongo|賀茂別雷神社|かもわけいかづちじんじゃ}}), is dedicated to [[Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]]. Locals refer to it as "West Shrine." The complex also includes a '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' dedicated to the mythical three-legged crow, {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|qid=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu|1=Yatagarasu}}. The shrine grounds are surrounded by ancient trees, including notable specimens designated as natural monuments. The site has been listed among Sendai's "100 Scenic Green Spots."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sendai.jp/ryokuchihozen/kurashi/shizen/midori/reiwa100sen/documents/92_kamojinja.pdf|title=Sendai's Scenic Green Spots: Kamo Shrine|publisher=Sendai City|accessdate=2024-11-08}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine, originally established on the estate of Shinto priests from {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|qid=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} in the {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|qid=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}. In 1607, the shrines were relocated to the grounds of Shiogama Shrine under the patronage of {{ill|Date Masamune|en|Date Masamune|qid=Q311183|lt=Date Masamune|1=Date Masamune}}, the first lord of the Sendai Domain. Later, during renovations of Shiogama Shrine under {{ill|Date Tsunamura|en|Date Tsunamura|qid=Q5227557|lt=Date Tsunamura|1=Date Tsunamura}}, the two shrines were moved to their current location in 1696 on the site of Furuchi Shima Yoshiyuki’s estate.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kamojinja.org/kamo/kamo_yui.htm|title=Kamo Shrine History|publisher=Kamo Shrine Official Site|accessdate=2024-11-08}} === Timeline === * **1607** – Relocated to the Shiogama Shrine precincts. * **1696** – Tadasu Shrine and Kifune Shrine were separately enshrined at the current site as Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines, respectively. == festivals == * **January 1** – New Year festival * **January 14** – {{ill|Donto Festival|ja|どんと祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Donto-sai|qq=}}, attracting approximately 30,000 visitors. * **April 29** – Spring festival * **July 31** – Summer Purification Ritual * **October 7** – Autumn festival * **November** – Shichi-Go-San (Children’s festival) == Cultural Properties == * **Miyagi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties** ** **main halls of Shimogamo and Kamigamo Shrines** – Designated in 1964. ** **Natural Monuments** *** Two Japanese maples (''Acer palmatum'') – Designated in 1999.{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/11TENNEN/ken/22momiji.htm|title=Japanese maples at Kamo Shrine|publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|accessdate=2024-11-08}} *** One Chinese evergreen oak (''Quercus glauca'') * **Sendai City Designated Natural Monuments** ** Two Chinese evergreen oaks – Designated in 1985. == Access == * **By Subway:** {{ill|Sendai Subway Namboku Line|en|Sendai Subway Namboku Line|qid=Q908156|lt=Sendai Subway Namboku Line|1=Sendai Subway Namboku Line}} to {{ill|Yaotome Station|en|Yaotome Station|qid=Q8049075|lt=Yaotome Station|1=Yaotome Station}}, followed by a short bus ride to "Kamo Shrine" bus stop. * **By Car:** Accessible from the [[:en:Tohoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] via the [[Izumi PA Smart IC]]. == Nearby Attractions == The shrine is located near other historic sites such as Chomeikan Castle ruins and Jigenji Temple, a former sub-temple of Shifukuji, one of Sendai’s Five Mountain Temples. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kamojinja.org/ Official Website of Kamo Shrine] * [https://www.city.sendai.jp/kensetsu/ryokka/midori100/guide/080/080.html Kamo Shrine on Sendai's Scenic Green Spots List] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (仙台市)|version=102499368|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:賀茂神社 (仙台市)]] {{Commons category|Kamo-jinja, Sendai}} {{wikidata link|Q11634948}} [[Category:Architecture in Izumi Ward, Sendai]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:History of Izumi Ward, Sendai]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Miyagi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sendai]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
